goal: Add a CLI flag --greet <name> that prints Hello, <name>! and keeps the current default output unchanged.
normalFlow: The command prints Hello, Alice! and exits 0.
exceptionFlow: User runs bash ./app.sh --greet without a name.
The command prints an error and exits non-zero.
coreOutcome: The new greeting flag works without changing existing default or unknown-option behavior.
successCriteria: bash ./app.sh keeps printing Hello from minimal target
